title: corylus avellana subsp. memorabilis
intro: Corylus avellana subsp. memorabilis, also known as the Hazelnut or Filbert, is a subspecies of the Hazel tree, native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Corylus avellana subsp. memorabilis include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and support cognitive function. This plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, which can be used to support its medicinal properties and health benefits.
